title: AE/CM/DV/AESAE forms live only on SE_COMMON
description: Every study MUST include one event/visit called 'Common Visit' with OID SE_COMMON (repeating, non-scheduled, available after the enrollment/randomization event). The forms AE (Adverse Events), CM (Concomitant Medications), DV (Protocol Deviations), and AESAE (Serious Adverse Event Report) MUST live ONLY on SE_COMMON: visits_assigned = ['SE_COMMON'], exactly that and nothing else. Skip SE_COMMON entirely only if none of those four forms are in scope.
rationale: AEs, CMs, deviations, and SAEs occur at any time during the trial, not at scheduled visits. Attaching them to every scheduled visit creates duplication, confuses the data model, and forces coordinators to choose a visit context that may not match when the event actually occurred. A single Common Visit gives coordinators one place to log cross-visit events and matches OpenClinica's native 'common event' pattern.

flag.message: Form ${form.form_id} pinned to SE_COMMON by global rule. Verify event SE_COMMON exists in study.events with event_type='common', is_repeating=true, available_after=<enrollment event oid>.

history.note: Imported from prompts.py RULE OC-9 during Phase B.1a. This is the most-cited rule in the audit — reaffirmed across nine System 4 vendor files. Runtime enforcement currently lives in pipeline.py:_enforce_common_visit; Phase C will remove that function in favor of engine application. The set directive carries the visits_assigned pinning; the SE_COMMON event creation (when absent) is delegated to the orchestrator since events are study-scoped, not form-scoped — a future DSL extension may add a 'set_at_study_scope' operator. Conditional skip when none of the four forms are in scope is handled at the orchestrator layer.
